video:video.video:title: Funeral held in Gaza for leader of Hamas’ military wing killed in Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: Mourners in the Gaza Strip held a funeral procession for the leader of Hamas’ military wing on Saturday after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike the previous day.

video:video.video:title: Long Island Rail Road shuts down as workers strike
video:video.video:description: A marathon day of negotiations that finished early Monday failed to end a strike that shut down the Long Island Rail Road, North America’s largest commuter rail system, setting the stage for a rough start to the work week for about 250,000 commuters.

video:video.video:title: Cate Blanchett: small acts are the key to freedom
video:video.video:description: Cate Blanchett calls for engagement in society and the rejection of fear-based narratives, as her Displacement Film Fund announces its 2026 cohort of recipients, in Cannes.

video:video.video:title: Commuters diverted to subways as LIRR strike disrupts millions
video:video.video:description: Workers got up extra early Monday to navigate a maze of car, bus and subway routes after a strike shut down the Long Island Railroad in New York, North America’s largest commuter rail system.

video:video.video:title: Floral festival takes over wealthy London neighbourhood
video:video.video:description: Shops and public spaces in London's affluent neighborhood are decorated in blooms for an annual celebration that runs parallel to the world-famous Chelsea Flower Show.

video:video.video:title: Africa CDC chief says he's in 'panic mode' over Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda
video:video.video:description: The absence of approved therapeutics or vaccines for the Bundibugyo virus, a rare variant of the Ebola disease, has caused concerns among health authorities in Africa amid the active cases.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: 'Pillion' star Harry Melling is ready to be transported back to Hogwarts
video:video.video:description: Harry Melling, who played Dudley Dursley in the original films, explains why he's looking forward to watching the new "Harry Potter" series. (May 18)

video:video.video:title: A tour of la Croisette
video:video.video:description: AP takes you on a tour of Boulevard de la Croisette - the beating heart of the annual Cannes Film Festival.

video:video.video:title: Long Island Rail Road shuts down as workers strike
video:video.video:description: A marathon day of negotiations that finished early Monday failed to end a strike that shut down the Long Island Rail Road, North America’s largest commuter rail system, setting the stage for a rough start to the work week for about 250,000 commuters.

video:video.video:title: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship docks in the Netherlands
video:video.video:description: The cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak has begun entering the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection, wrapping up a troubled journey that put international health authorities on alert.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 17
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Sunday, May 17th: WHO declares an Ebola emergency; A drone strike hits the UAE’s sole nuclear plant; Russian officials say Ukrainian strikes killed four people; An owl sanctuary in Spain promotes wildlife conservation.

video:video.video:title: Bulgaria welcomes home Eurovision winner Dara after historic victory
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of fans waving Bulgarian flags gathered at Sofia Airport as the singer arrived carrying the Eurovision trophy after winning with her song “Bangaranga.”

video:video.video:title: Peru's electoral board confirms June 7 presidential runoff with Fujimori and Sánchez
video:video.video:description: Peruvian electoral authorities confirmed on Sunday the official results of the first round of the presidential elections in early April, with Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sánchez advancing to the runoff on June 7.

video:video.video:title: Three Palestinians killed in Israeli strike on Gaza soup kitchen as bodies brought to hospital
video:video.video:description: Three Palestinians were killed in an Israeli strike on a soup kitchen in central Gaza on Sunday, according to officials at the hospital where the bodies were brought.

video:video.video:title: Iraqi special forces take journalists to site of reported Israeli outpost
video:video.video:description: The Iraqi military took journalists to the site of a reported Israeli outpost in the desert on Sunday to show there was no indication of a long-term military presence at the site.

video:video.video:title: Judge allows gun and notebook as evidence at Mangione’s trial in UnitedHealthcare CEO’s killing
video:video.video:description: The judge allowed evidence obtained during a subsequent inventory of his backpack at the police station, but said evidence found during an initial search of Mangione’s backpack during his arrest at a McDonald’s restaurant must be suppressed.

video:video.video:title: Workers form picket lines as strike shuts down the largest US commuter rail system
video:video.video:description: The strike shutting down North America’s largest commuter rail system will continue into a third day.

video:video.video:title: Residents of Bulgaria capital are pleased following Dara's Eurovision victory and grand welcome home
video:video.video:description: Residents of Sofia reacted with excitement after Bulgarian singer, Dara, won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: 'Extraordinary Attorney Woo' star Park Eun-bin shares career secrets
video:video.video:description: South Korean actor Park Eun-bin explains what has kept her going through 30 years in the industry, from child model to her latest role in "The WONDERfools." (May 18)

video:video.video:title: Argentina's southernmost outpost of Ushuaia fears hantavirus speculation will chill tourism
video:video.video:description: The Associated Press' Isabel Debre takes a look at how the Argentine wind-lashed city of Ushuaia that bills itself as the “end of the world” now fears for its future after it found itself at the center of speculation about the source of a deadly hantavirus outbreak.

video:video.video:title: Rep. Raskin calls Trump's $1.7B IRS settlement his latest 'slush fund'
video:video.video:description: The lead Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee is calling the Trump administration's creation of a $1.7 billion fund to compensate allies of the Republican president who believe they were mistreated by the Biden-era Justice Department a "slush fund."

video:video.video:title: Two Navy jets collide during Idaho air show
video:video.video:description: An air show in Idaho was called off when two Navy jets collided and crashed. The four crew members from both jets safely ejected and the crash is under investigation.

video:video.video:title: Protesters light bonfires during public transport strike in Kenya over fuel prices
video:video.video:description: Protests have erupted in Nairobi as a nationwide public transport strike begins over rising fuel prices. (AP video shot by Andrew Kasuku)

video:video.video:title: Police on Long Island deploy pepper spray on crowd lining up for high demand luxury watch
video:video.video:description: A scuffle erupted between deputies and watch enthusiasts who had camped outside a New York store on Saturday, prompting officers to deploy pepper spray on the crowd.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 18
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, May 18th: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship docks in Rotterdam; Luigi Mangione in court for evidence hearing; Turkey's Erdogan denounces Israeli navy's interception of Gaza-bound flotilla; Epstein files exhibition opens in New York City.

video:video.video:title: Two suspects killed in shooting at a San Diego mosque
video:video.video:description: The two suspects in a Monday shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego are dead, according to a police source.

video:video.video:title: Fast-growing brush fire in Southern California threatens homes
video:video.video:description: The fire was reported Monday just after 10 a.m. in Simi Valley, a city in Ventura County northwest of Los Angeles.

video:video.video:title: Iran soccer team departs for Turkey training camp ahead of World Cup
video:video.video:description: Iran's semi-official Tasnim news agency posted a video showing members of the country's soccer team signing Jerseys ahead of their trip.

video:video.video:title: Israeli troops intercept vessels from a flotilla trying to breach the blockade of Gaza
video:video.video:description: More than 50 vessels departed from the port in Marmaris, Turkey, last week in what the organizers of the Global Sumud Flotilla described as the final leg of their planned journey to Gaza’s shores.

video:video.video:title: Adam Driver responds to Lena Dunham
video:video.video:description: Actor Adam Driver kept tight lipped when asked if he had a response for Lena Dunham after comments made about him by her in her book.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 18
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, May 18th: Trump and Iran send new warnings; Ukrainian drone strikes near Moscow; South Carolina to take up new redistricting measure; New York’s Long Island Railroad on strike.

video:video.video:title: Adam Driver responds to Lena Dunham
video:video.video:description: Actor Adam Driver kept tight lipped when asked if he had a response for Lena Dunham after comments made about him by her in her book. (May 18)

video:video.video:title: Hundreds attend anti-Israeli government rally in Tel Aviv
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of Israeli demonstrators attended an anti-government rally in Tel Aviv on Saturday evening.

video:video.video:title: What a hoot: Come meet the rescue owls in a Spanish sanctuary
video:video.video:description: A sanctuary in southern Spain is giving injured and rescued owls a second chance at life. Visitors can get unusually close to some of Europe's most striking birds of prey.

video:video.video:title: Indigenous leaders say the US-Mexico border wall is ruining sacred sites
video:video.video:description: Indigenous leaders say U.S. government contractors in the rush to expand the wall are desecrating Native American sacred places and cultural sites at an unprecedented pace.

video:video.video:title: North Korean women's soccer team makes rare visit to South Korea for tournament
video:video.video:description: A North Korean women’s soccer team arrived in South Korea on Sunday for the semifinals of the AFC Women’s Champions League.

video:video.video:title: The UK’s last silk ribbon weavers keep their craft alive
video:video.video:description: Silk ribbon weaving was once a major UK industry, back when ribbons were a symbol of status and going out to buy them was considered a popular pastime for Georgian and Victorian women.

video:video.video:title: Small farmers in a bind after Hawaii's worst flooding in 20 years
video:video.video:description: Hawaii's recent catastrophic flooding has devastated local farms, leaving many farmers struggling and setting back the state's efforts to bolster a local food supply.

video:video.video:title: NY governor begs unions to resume talks as North America’s largest commuter rail system shut down
video:video.video:description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul begged unions representing workers for the Long Island Rail Road, North America’s largest commuter rail system, to resume bargaining Sunday, saying the workers and hundreds of thousands of commuters stand to lose if a strike drags on.

video:video.video:title: Doctor identified among Pompeii victims thanks to analysis of the case he was carrying
video:video.video:description: One of the victims of the eruption that buried Pompeii in 79 AD. has been identified as a doctor thanks to analysis carried out on a small case that had remained hidden on them.

video:video.video:title: Drone strike sparks fire at UAE nuclear power plant in latest blow to Iran ceasefire
video:video.video:description: A drone strike targeted the United Arab Emirates’ sole nuclear power plant on Sunday, sparking a fire on its perimeter.

video:video.video:title: DRC health minister, WHO representatives arrive in Bunia with supplies to fight Ebola outbreak
video:video.video:description: DRC health minister Samuel Roger Kamba said on Sunday that authorities had already selected sites in Rwampara and Mongwalu to establish new treatment centers and that dozens of patients were receiving active care.

video:video.video:title: 4 crew members eject safely after two Navy jets collide during air show in Idaho
video:video.video:description: All four crew members ejected safely after two Navy jets collided Sunday during an air show at the Mountain Home Air Force Base in western Idaho, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong court hears final arguments in former Tiananmen vigil organizers' national security trial
video:video.video:description: Chow Hang-tung and Lee Cheuk-yan, two former leaders of the Hong Kong Alliance in Support of Patriotic Democratic Movements of China, have pleaded not guilty.

video:video.video:title: Halsey is turning her attention to film
video:video.video:description: Grammy-nominated artist Halsey explains why she's at the Cannes Film Festival, including to network for her freshly announced horror, "Replacer." (May 18)

video:video.video:title: Michigan student will be the first woman to represent the US in world welding competition
video:video.video:description: A Michigan community college student will be the first female welder to represent the United States at a worldwide competition described as the Olympics of the skilled trades.

video:video.video:title: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship docks in the Netherlands for disinfection as final crew disembark
video:video.video:description: A cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak docked at the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection Monday, wrapping up a troubled journey that put international health authorities on alert.

video:video.video:title: White House official says there are 'no cases of Ebola in America'
video:video.video:description: At a White House event about prescription drugs with President Donald Trump, Heidi Overton, a medical doctor who is deputy director of the White House Domestic Policy Council, was on hand to address any questions from reporters about the Ebola infections.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: Bolivia protesters allied with ex-leader Morales march on capital as unrest widens
video:video.video:description: Followers of Bolivia’s influential ex-President Evo Morales on Monday joined a protest movement fueled by the nation’s worst economic crisis in a generation.

video:video.video:title: Italy's president and prime minister visit Modena the day after car attack
video:video.video:description: Italian President, Sergio Mattarella, and Prime Minister, Giorgia Meloni, visited a hospital on Sunday treating those injured after a car collided with pedestrians in the city of Modena.

video:video.video:title: 'I consider myself lucky': Survivor recounts Modena car attack
video:video.video:description: A man injured in a car-ramming and stabbing attack in Italy’s northern city of Modena said on Monday he considered himself lucky not to have been more seriously injured, and thanked those who came to his aid.

video:video.video:title: Armed members of Iran's nomadic Bakhtiari tribe march in Tehran amid tensions with US
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of members of Iran's Bakhtiari tribe gathered in Tehran on Monday to pledge allegiance to Iran's supreme leader. (AP Video by Mohsen Ganji)

video:video.video:title: Alex Murdaugh sues clerk whose behavior led state Supreme Court to overturn his murder convictions
video:video.video:description: The legal defense team for Alex Murdaugh announced a lawsuit against the court clerk whose behavior during his murder trial led the South Carolina Supreme Court to overturn his convictions and life sentence for the deaths of his wife and son.

video:video.video:title: Clashes erupt in Bolivia as protesters allied with ex-leader Morales march on capital
video:video.video:description: Followers of Bolivia’s influential ex-President Evo Morales on Monday joined a massive protest movement fueled by the nation’s worst economic crisis in a generation.

video:video.video:title: Brazilian artisan crafts replica World Cup trophies to the joy of locals and celebrities alike
video:video.video:description: Brazilian soccer fan and former metallurgist Jarbas Meneghini Carlini has been combining his love for the sport with his business, making replicas of the FIFA World Cup trophy in his workshop in western Rio de Janeiro.

video:video.video:title: Robert Downey Jr. says live theater is 'vital'
video:video.video:description: Robert Downey, Jr. says live theater is "vital" as he and his wife, producer Susan Downey, are honored at Lincoln Center Theater's annual gala. (May 18)

video:video.video:title: Russia's Putin to visit China days after Trump met with President Xi
video:video.video:description: Russia's President Vladimir Putin will visit China Tuesday and Wednesday this week, the Chinese Foreign Minister announced.

video:video.video:title: Ebola outbreak designated global health emergency by WHO with Congo to open three treatment centers
video:video.video:description: The Democratic Republic of Congo will open three Ebola treatment centers in the eastern province of Ituri after an outbreak of a rare strain of the virus that has no approved vaccines or treatments, as international health officials warned of a growing risk of regional spread.

video:video.video:title: Protests in Venezuela after elderly mother dies days after learning missing son died in prison
video:video.video:description: Protesters demonstrated Monday in Venezuela’s capital in memory of a woman who died over the weekend, just days after learning that her son had died in state custody 10 months ago.

video:video.video:title: Ship with aid from Mexico and Uruguay sails into Havana port
video:video.video:description: A ship carrying humanitarian aid from Mexico and Uruguay arrived in Havana Monday as Cuba faces worsening shortages of food, fuel and electricity.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy claims a 'significant' number of Russian regions are in a state of bankruptcy
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Monday claimed a "significant number" of Russian regions were in a state of bankruptcy.

video:video.video:title: Professional mourners mix tribal tradition with Kenya’s widespread Christianity
video:video.video:description: Loud and expressive mourners are common at funerals around the world—but in parts of Kenya, some of them may be paid workers.

video:video.video:title: King Charles and Queen Camilla attend annual Chelsea Flower Show in London
video:video.video:description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla visited London’s renowned Chelsea Flower Show Monday.

video:video.video:title: A third 'Sherlock' film for Robert Downey Jr?
video:video.video:description: Robert Downey Jr. and his producer wife Susan Downey aren't ready to confirm that a third "Sherlock Holmes" film is in the works, calling it "the greatest mystery."

video:video.video:title: Nearby resident recounts San Diego mosque shooting
video:video.video:description: Two teenage gunmen opened fire at an Islamic Center in San Diego on Monday and killed three men before killing themselves a few blocks away, police said.

video:video.video:title: Village festival combines catching fish with clearing weeds and silt
video:video.video:description: Villagers wade through a spring covered with algae, clearing the weeds. It's a century old tradition in the Himalayan region of Indian-controlled Kashmir that combines environmental conservation with a community celebration.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he put off 'very major attack' on Iran Tuesday after 'big discussions' with Tehran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said he is postponing a "major" planned military strike on Iran Tuesday at the request of Middle East allies because “serious negotiations” are underway, saying he hopes the delay lasts “for a little while, hopefully, maybe forever."

video:video.video:title: Shooting at San Diego mosque kills 3 men and both suspects are dead, police say
video:video.video:description: A shooting at a San Diego mosque on Monday killed three men, and two teenage suspects are also dead, the police chief said.

video:video.video:title: Police: Two suspects kill 3 people at a San Diego mosque before killing themselves
video:video.video:description: Authorities say two teenage suspects opened fire at an Islamic center in San Diego, California, killing three men before taking their own lives.

video:video.video:title: San Diego police say they are responding to an active shooter at a local Islamic Center
video:video.video:description: San Diego police are responding to an active shooter at the Islamic Center of San Diego. Officer Anthony Carrasco says they believe people have been shot.

video:video.video:title: How do global events affect gas prices at the pump?
video:video.video:description: Gas prices move up and down in response to disruptions in supply and spikes in demand. Many global events such as conflicts, sanctions or decisions by major oil producing countries can limit or create uncertainty about the supply of oil.

video:video.video:title: Trump holding off on Iran military strike as 'serious negotiations' underway
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump said he is holding off on a military strike on Iran planned for Tuesday because “serious negotiations” are underway to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Former LA police detective Mark Fuhrman, key figure in OJ Simpson murder trial, dies
video:video.video:description: Former Los Angeles police detective Mark Fuhrman, who was convicted of lying during testimony at the O.J. Simpson murder trial, has died. He was 74.

video:video.video:title: Negotiators reach a deal to end strike on North America’s busiest commuter rail system
video:video.video:description: Negotiators have reached a deal to end a strike that has brought North America’s largest commuter rail system to a standstill.

video:video.video:title: Interfaith march in Jerusalem's Old City seeks to provide show of unity
video:video.video:description: Dozens of activists participated in an interfaith march through Jerusalem on Monday. (AP Video by Shlomo Mor)

video:video.video:title: Nicholas Galitzine says playing He-Man was something he had to 'earn'
video:video.video:description: At the premiere of “Masters of the Universe,” Nicholas Galitzine said playing He-Man was a role he felt he had to “earn.” The film hits theaters June 5. (May 18)

video:video.video:title: Putin says Russia-China ties at ‘unprecedented level’ ahead of China visit
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Russia-China ties have reached a “truly unprecedented level” in a video address released by the Kremlin on Tuesday, ahead of his visit to China for talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.
